samples.xml_description:
Implement an unsigned 8bit multiplier based on pipelining processing. Clock signal used for synchronous operation. Active-low reset signal. Defined as 0 for chip reset and 1 for reset signal inactive. Input enable signal that controls the multiplication operation. Input signal representing the multiplicand with a data width of 8 bits. Input signal representing the multiplier with a data width of 8 bits. Output enable signal indicating if the multiplication operation is enabled. Product output signal with a data width of 16 bits. 8 16 A register to store the multiplication enable signal. A register to store the multiplicand. A register to store the multiplier. A register to store the sum. A register to store the output. A wire to store the intermediate result. A wire to store the result of the AND operation. A wire to store the carry-in.

mul_en_out is the output enable signal that indicates whether the multiplication operation is enabled.

On the positive edge of the clock signal, the input enable signal, mul_en_in, is sampled and stored in the register, mul_en_out_reg.

The output enable signal, mul_en_out, is derived from the most significant bit of mul_en_out_reg.

assign mul_en_out = mul_en_out_reg[N-1];

mul_a_reg is a register to store the multiplicand.

On the positive edge of the clock signal, the multiplicand, mul_a, is sampled and stored in the register, mul_a_reg.

The register is only updated when the input enable signal is active.

always @(posedge clk) begin

    if (mul_en_in) begin

      mul_a_reg[7:0] <= mul_a[7:0];

    end

end

mul_b_reg is a register to store the multiplier.

On the positive edge of the clock signal, the multiplier, mul_b, is sampled and stored in the register, mul_b_reg.

The register is only updated when the input enable signal is active.

always @(posedge clk) begin

    if (mul_en_in) begin

      mul_b_reg[7:0] <= mul_b[7:0];

    end

end

Partial Product Generation

For each bit position, the partial product is generated by ANDing the multiplicand with the corresponding multiplier bit.

The result of the AND operation is stored in the wire, temp.

genvar i;

generate

    for (i = 0; i < N; i = i + 1) begin : gen_loop

      assign temp[i] = mul_a_reg[i] && mul_b_reg[N-1-i];

    end

endgenerate

Partial Sum Calculation

For each group of partial products, the partial sum is calculated by adding the bits together.

The result of the addition is stored in the register, sum.

always @(posedge clk) begin

    if (rst_n) begin

      sum[0] <= temp[0];

      for (i = 1; i < N; i = i + 1) begin : add_loop

          sum[i] <= temp[i] + sum[i-1];

      end

    end

end

Final Product Calculation

The final product is calculated by adding all the partial sums together.

The result of the addition is stored in the register, mul_out_reg.

always @(posedge clk) begin

    if (rst_n) begin

      mul_out_reg[
